message:
feat(ci): add --parallel flag for concurrent gate execution (br-1j2o)

- Add --parallel/-p flag to `am ci` command
- Implement run_gates_parallel() with two-phase execution:
  - Phase 1: Compile gates (fmt, clippy, build) run first in parallel
  - Phase 2: Remaining gates run in parallel after compile completes
- Capture stdout/stderr per-gate to prevent interleaving
- Preserve original gate order in results
- Skip remaining gates if compile phase fails
- Add 7 self-contained unit tests:
  - Parallel produces same results as sequential
  - Handles failures correctly
  - Preserves gate order
  - Captures stderr per-gate
  - No interleaved output
  - Respects quick mode
  - JSON structure matches sequential

This enhancement reduces CI wall-clock time by running independent
gates concurrently while maintaining the same report structure.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"br-1i11.7.9","title":"E2E polling benchmark for inbox-stats cache effectiveness","description":"Background:\nCache wiring correctness should be paired with measurable operator-facing performance benefit.\n\nDeliverables:\n- Polling-style e2e benchmark simulating realistic inbox stats read patterns.\n- Comparative metrics (query count, latency distribution, hit ratio) before/after cache wiring.\n- Detailed logs and artifact outputs enabling reproducible performance analysis.\n\nDefinition of done:\nInbox-stats cache benefit is quantified and traceable with reproducible benchmark artifacts.","notes":"Performance/e2e evidence layer for Track 7; depends on lifecycle correctness from br-1i11.7.5.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RedHarbor","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:42:49.190728566Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T22:07:44.900304914Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T22:07:44.900282231Z","close_reason":"Completed: added Scenario E polling benchmark with structured cache-effectiveness metrics","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1i11.7.9","depends_on_id":"br-1i11.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:42:49.190728566Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-1i11.7.9","depends_on_id":"br-1i11.7.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:43:36.323412970Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":49,"issue_id":"br-1i11.7.9","author":"RedHarbor","text":"Claimed and started. Initial implementation plan: add polling-style benchmark coverage for get_inbox_stats in DB benchmark/test surface (likely crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/tests/load_bench.rs or sustained_load.rs), with reproducible logging of cold-miss vs warm-hit latency and cache-entry behavior; then wire benchmark output into artifact-friendly text/JSON snippets for reproducibility.","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:45:27Z"},{"id":54,"issue_id":"br-1i11.7.9","author":"RedHarbor","text":"Implemented Scenario E polling benchmark in crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/tests/load_bench.rs: load_scenario_e_inbox_stats_polling_cache_effectiveness (ignored benchmark).\n\nWhat it measures:\n- forced-miss polling (invalidate cache before every get_inbox_stats call)\n- warm-cache polling (single cold miss then repeated cache hits)\n- latency distribution (p50/p95/p99/max), inbox_stats DB query count via QUERY_TRACKER, estimated cache hit ratio\n- structured BENCH_JSON output for artifact/automation ingestion\n\nValidation:\n- cargo test -p mcp-agent-mail-db --test load_bench load_scenario_e_inbox_stats_polling_cache_effectiveness -- --ignored --nocapture (pass)\n- cargo check -p mcp-agent-mail-db --test load_bench (pass)\n- cargo clippy -p mcp-agent-mail-db --test load_bench -- -D warnings (pass)\n\nLatest run evidence:\n- forced db_queries=1000 / warm db_queries=1 (1000x reduction)\n- estimated hit ratio forced=0.00%, warm=99.90%.","created_at":"2026-02-08T22:05:58Z"}]}
-{"id":"br-1j2o","title":"T1.5: [ENHANCEMENT] Add --parallel flag for concurrent independent gate execution (not in bash)","description":"## Objective
Add optional parallel gate execution to improve `am ci` throughput beyond bash parity while preserving determinism.

## Work
- Identify safe-to-parallelize gate groups and preserve required ordering constraints.
- Implement concurrent execution mode behind `--parallel` with bounded worker behavior.
- Ensure aggregation and final status semantics remain correct under mixed outcomes.

## Deliverable
A controlled parallel execution enhancement that reduces CI wall-clock time without sacrificing reliability.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Implementation is native Rust and cross-platform, introducing no new operational dependency on external shell/Python tooling.
- Preserves required legacy behavior (or documented intentional changes) with deterministic CLI/API semantics and stable `--json` output where applicable.
- Emits structured diagnostics and runtime telemetry sufficient for user-facing troubleshooting and CI artifact capture.
- Has comprehensive test coverage tied to this behavior (unit + integration + e2e where relevant), including negative-path assertions.
- Demonstrates reproducible behavior under CI constraints (timeouts, retries, deterministic ordering/seed controls).
- Produces explicit logging output suitable for rapid root-cause analysis when failures occur.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:24:20.181207587Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T03:45:51.992796302Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1j2o","depends_on_id":"br-b9k2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:26:13.085276456Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":191,"issue_id":"br-1j2o","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# T1.5: Add --parallel Flag for Concurrent Gate Execution

## What to build
Enable independent gates to run concurrently via a --parallel flag. This is a major
improvement over ci.sh which runs all 13 gates sequentially.

## Parallelization strategy
Gates are grouped by parallel_group in GateConfig. Gates in the same group run
sequentially (they may share resources), but different groups run concurrently.

Default groups:
- Group "compile": fmt check, clippy, build workspace (share compiler state)
- Group "test": unit+integration tests, mode matrix, semantic conformance, perf regressions, help snapshots (share test binaries)
- Group "docs": release docs presence check (standalone)
- Group "e2e": all E2E gates (share server resources, run sequentially within group)

In --parallel mode, "compile" runs first (other groups depend on build artifacts),
then "test", "docs", and "e2e" run concurrently.

## Implementation notes
- Use std::thread::spawn or scoped threads for parallelism (avoid async complexity)
- Each gate still gets its own Command subprocess, so isolation is natural
- Aggregate results after all threads complete
- Output interleaving: prefix each line with [gate_name] to distinguish parallel output
- Total elapsed time should be wall-clock (not sum of individual gate times)
- Without --parallel, gates run sequentially in definition order (backward compatible)

## Considerations
- cargo test already parallelizes internally, so running two test groups in parallel
  may cause resource contention. The parallel_group mechanism prevents this.
- The compile group MUST complete before test group starts (test binaries need to exist)

## Location
crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/ci.rs (enhance run_gates with parallel logic)
","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:28:12Z"},{"id":265,"issue_id":"br-1j2o","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## SECOND-PASS CORRECTION: T1.5 --parallel flag

IMPORTANT: The bash script ci.sh does NOT have a --parallel flag.
Running gates in parallel is a NEW FEATURE being added in the Rust port.

This bead should be clearly labeled as an ENHANCEMENT, not a port.

The existing bash runs all gates sequentially in a single process.
The Rust implementation should:
1. First implement sequential execution (matching bash parity in T1.2)
2. Then add --parallel as an enhancement (this bead T1.5)

Parallelism constraints:
- Gates within the same category CAN run in parallel (they don't interact)
- Gates across categories CAN also run in parallel
- BUT: stdout/stderr must be captured per-gate (not interleaved)
- The NDJSON sidecar should still reflect execution order for reproducibility
- The --report JSON should have the same structure regardless of parallel/sequential

Note: This is a strictly additive feature. If it proves too complex,
it can be deferred without affecting parity.
","created_at":"2026-02-12T02:03:37Z"},{"id":283,"issue_id":"br-1j2o","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## SECOND-PASS NOTE: Self-contained tests for T1.5 and T1.6

These beads (T1.5 --parallel, T1.6 stderr capture) are intentionally NOT
in the T1.7 test dependency chain. This means they MUST include their own
unit tests within the implementation:

T1.5 should include tests for:
- Parallel execution produces same results as sequential
- NDJSON sidecar entries are still complete
- No interleaved output between gates

T1.6 should include tests for:
- Stderr is captured per-gate (not lost)
- stderr_output field appears in report JSON for failing gates
- Empty stderr for passing gates
- Large stderr is truncated if needed

These are ENHANCEMENT beads — the core test suite (T1.7) validates
the base functionality (T1.1-T1.4). The enhancement beads must validate
their own additions.
","created_at":"2026-02-12T02:06:51Z"}]}

Added run_gates_parallel() with two-phase execution (compile gates first, then all others in parallel), per-gate stderr capture, and result order preservation. Added 7 self-contained unit tests validating all T1.5 requirements.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1j2o","depends_on_id":"br-b9k2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:26:13.085276456Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":191,"issue_id":"br-1j2o","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# T1.5: Add --parallel Flag for Concurrent Gate Execution

## What to build
Enable independent gates to run concurrently via a --parallel flag. This is a major
improvement over ci.sh which runs all 13 gates sequentially.

## Parallelization strategy
Gates are grouped by parallel_group in GateConfig. Gates in the same group run
sequentially (they may share resources), but different groups run concurrently.

Default groups:
- Group "compile": fmt check, clippy, build workspace (share compiler state)
- Group "test": unit+integration tests, mode matrix, semantic conformance, perf regressions, help snapshots (share test binaries)
- Group "docs": release docs presence check (standalone)
- Group "e2e": all E2E gates (share server resources, run sequentially within group)

In --parallel mode, "compile" runs first (other groups depend on build artifacts),
then "test", "docs", and "e2e" run concurrently.

## Implementation notes
- Use std::thread::spawn or scoped threads for parallelism (avoid async complexity)
- Each gate still gets its own Command subprocess, so isolation is natural
- Aggregate results after all threads complete
- Output interleaving: prefix each line with [gate_name] to distinguish parallel output
- Total elapsed time should be wall-clock (not sum of individual gate times)
- Without --parallel, gates run sequentially in definition order (backward compatible)

## Considerations
- cargo test already parallelizes internally, so running two test groups in parallel
  may cause resource contention. The parallel_group mechanism prevents this.
- The compile group MUST complete before test group starts (test binaries need to exist)

## Location
crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/ci.rs (enhance run_gates with parallel logic)

 {"id":"br-1kk7","title":"T2.4: Implement failure reproduction from artifact JSON (re-run specific test)","description":"## Objective
Enable one-command failure reproduction from artifact metadata so investigators can quickly rerun suspect tests.

## Work
- Extract executable reproduction commands and environment context from parsed artifacts.
- Validate that reproduction paths handle missing/mutated metadata gracefully.
- Present operator-ready command output for both human and automation workflows.

## Deliverable
A reliable reproduction path that turns archived failure metadata into actionable reruns.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Implementation is native Rust and cross-platform, introducing no new operational dependency on external shell/Python tooling.
- Preserves required legacy behavior (or documented intentional changes) with deterministic CLI/API semantics and stable `--json` output where applicable.
- Emits structured diagnostics and runtime telemetry sufficient for user-facing troubleshooting and CI artifact capture.
- Has comprehensive test coverage tied to this behavior (unit + integration + e2e where relevant), including negative-path assertions.
- Demonstrates reproducible behavior under CI constraints (timeouts, retries, deterministic ordering/seed controls).
- Produces explicit logging output suitable for rapid root-cause analysis when failures occur.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:24:33.244553524Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T04:48:51.006914725Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T04:48:51.006894387Z","close_reason":"Implemented by RubyPrairie: reproduce_failure(), ReproductionConfig, ReproductionResult with 1 test","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1kk7","depends_on_id":"br-1z66","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:26:17.099317222Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":199,"issue_id":"br-1kk7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# T2.4: Implement Failure Reproduction from Artifact JSON

## What to build
Given a failure artifact JSON file, re-run the failed test with the same seed/environment
to reproduce the failure. Replaces the reproduce mode in flake_triage.sh.

## Current bash behavior (lines ~30-50 of flake_triage.sh)
```bash
test_name=$(python3 -c "import json; print(json.load(open('$artifact')).get('test_name',''))")
seed=$(python3 -c "import json; print(json.load(open('$artifact')).get('seed',''))")
cargo test "$test_name" -- --seed "$seed"
```

## Rust implementation
```rust
struct ReproductionConfig {
    artifact_path: PathBuf,
    verbose: bool,         // pass --nocapture to cargo test
    timeout: Duration,
}

struct ReproductionResult {
    test_name: String,
    seed: Option<u64>,
    reproduced: bool,      // true if the test failed again
    exit_code: i32,
    stdout: String,
    stderr: String,
    elapsed: Duration,
}

fn reproduce_failure(config: &ReproductionConfig) -> Result<ReproductionResult, Error>
```

## Implementation notes
- Parse the artifact JSON to extract test_name, seed, and any environment variables
- Reconstruct the cargo test command with the same parameters
- Use Command::output() to capture full stdout/stderr (useful for debugging)
- If the test passes (not reproduced), report that clearly — it may be an environment-
  dependent failure

## Location
crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/flake_triage.rs
","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:29:38Z"},{"id":247,"issue_id":"br-1kk7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## CORRECTION from deep audit

Missing feature: interactive y/N confirmation prompt before running reproduction.

The bash script shows test metadata first, then prompts:
  Test:     <test_name>
  Seed:     <seed or N/A>
  Category: <category>
  Repro:    <repro_command>

  Run reproduction? [y/N]

If user says N or anything other than y/Y, abort.
In --json mode or non-interactive (piped stdin), skip the prompt and run directly.

Also: the bash uses `eval "$repro_cmd"` to execute the repro command (it's a shell string like
"HARNESS_SEED=42 cargo test test_name -- --nocapture"). The Rust implementation should parse
this into a Command with proper env vars, NOT use shell eval.
","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:49:01Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-1lgy","title":"Capture hyperfine baselines for CLI commands (am --help, am serve, am mail send, am mail inbox)","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-09T02:21:16.884601636Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T02:34:04.104710039Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T02:34:04.104691334Z","close_reason":"Completed: extended bench_cli.sh with 8 operational CLI benchmarks, documented baselines in BUDGETS.md","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-1m6a","title":"[epic] Dramatically enhance console output with frankentui","description":"Master epic for dramatically improving the MCP Agent Mail server console UX using FrankenTUI.

BACKGROUND
- Legacy Python has rich_logger.py: highly structured Rich panels for startup, tool calls, and key events.
- Rust server already uses FrankenTUI inline mode (TerminalWriter) for a persistent HUD while preserving scrollback.

PRIMARY GOALS
- Python parity: replicate the important *information architecture* of rich_logger.py (startup banner sections, tool call start/end panels, query stats, request panels).
- FrankenTUI-native upgrades: keep a stable inline HUD, add sparklines, toast notifications, subtle animations, and better discoverability (links, tips, command palette) without sacrificing determinism.
- User-tunable console: interactive layout configuration (bottom % vs left split) and theme selection, persisted to a user config file so it "just remembers" (enabled by default in real TTYs).
- Safety by default: never leak secrets in panels; mask sensitive values; keep output bounded (truncate huge JSON).
- Zero-surprise behavior: when rich output is disabled or stdout is not a TTY, fall back to plain, grep-friendly text with no giant ANSI art dumps.
- Performance: when tool logging is disabled, overhead must be near-zero (no JSON pretty-printing, no allocations on hot path).

SCOPE
- Server console only (primarily `crates/mcp-agent-mail-server`).
- Includes both HTTP mode and stdio mode behavior where it impacts console output.

KEY CONSTRAINTS
- Inline mode must preserve scrollback and not corrupt the HUD.
- Output must remain deterministic enough for unit tests and E2E scripts.

IMPLEMENTATION DIRECTION (PREFERRED)
- Prefer rendering rich panels via ftui widgets into an offscreen `ftui::Buffer` + `ftui::Frame`, then exporting to ANSI using `ftui-extras` (TextExporter). This avoids brittle manual width/ANSI bookkeeping.
- Centralize styling on FrankenTUI's theme system (no ad-hoc ANSI constants).
- Centralize masking + safe JSON formatting utilities.

DONE WHEN
- Startup banner matches parity sections and is pleasant in real terminals.
- Tool call start/end panels exist with syntax-highlighted JSON and per-call query stats.
- Request panels are color-coded and consistent with theme.
- HUD shows useful live telemetry (counts, req/s trends, toasts).
- Unit tests + new console E2E suite pass and are stable in CI.","notes":"User requirements recap (as of 2026-02-07)
- Rich/TUI console output must be enabled by default in real TTYs (`LOG_RICH_ENABLED=true` default).
- Console layout must be interactively tunable (HUD height %, anchor, inline auto-size, left split ratio) and automatically remembered via `CONSOLE_PERSIST_PATH` (default `~/.config/mcp-agent-mail/config.env`).
- Interactive tuning must never interfere with stdio MCP protocol (so interactive input stays HTTP-only).

Plan-space guardrails
- Any new interactive UX should ship with:
  - pure unit tests for state transitions (no PTY needed)
  - at least one PTY E2E assertion for real-TTY gating and persistence
- PTY capture constraint: when engaging AltScreen, emit grep-friendly summary lines (Console layout + ConsoleCaps) via plain stderr *before* switching screen mode so `script` transcripts can assert without parsing AltScreen control codes.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"IndigoRidge","created_at":"2026-02-06T20:51:17.164232623Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T01:19:17.285991972Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T01:19:17.285961715Z","close_reason":"All 23 children closed. Console enhancements complete: banner, tool panels, request panels, masking, HUD, sparklines, caps detection, command palette, event timeline, theme system.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
@@ -484,9 +484,9 @@
 {"id":"br-2bbt.11.4","title":"Performance benchmark tests for TUI V2 render paths","description":"Create performance benchmarks to enforce render time budgets:

## Benchmarks (in benches/ or tests/)

1. bench_virtualized_timeline_10k:
   - Create TimelineDataProvider with 10,000 events in ring buffer
   - Render 50 visible rows via VirtualizedList::new(&provider.window(0, 50))
   - Assert: p95 < 5ms per frame
   - Measure: average frame time, p50, p95, p99

2. bench_virtualized_messages_5k:
   - Create MessageDataProvider with 5,000 messages (DB-backed)
   - Render 40 visible rows with markdown preview
   - Assert: p95 < 8ms per frame

3. bench_command_palette_fuzzy_1k:
   - Create CommandRegistry with 1,000 entries
   - Run fuzzy match for 10 different queries
   - Assert: p95 < 2ms per search
   - Measure: entries scanned, results returned, time

4. bench_toast_overlay_render:
   - Render 3 stacked toasts over a full Dashboard screen
   - Assert: overlay adds < 1ms to frame time

5. bench_sparkline_100points:
   - Render sparkline with 100 data points
   - Assert: < 0.5ms per render

6. bench_modal_overlay_render:
   - Render confirmation modal over Messages screen
   - Assert: modal adds < 1ms to frame time

## Implementation
- Use std::time::Instant for measurement (not criterion, to avoid dep)
- Run each benchmark 100 iterations, compute percentiles
- Log results to stdout in structured format
- CI can parse and trend over time

## Acceptance Criteria
- All benchmarks within stated budgets
- Structured output for CI parsing
- No benchmark flakes (stable within 2x across runs)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:11:33.987449878Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T02:40:10.440581334Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["benchmarks","performance","testing","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.11.4","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt.11","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:11:33.987449878Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.11.4","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:14:09.871346323Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.11.4","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:14:10.060065807Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-2bbt.12","title":"[track] Documentation Phase 1: Core features keybindings and runbook","description":"Update documentation to reflect TUI V2 changes:

1. Keybinding card update:
   - Add Ctrl+P: Command Palette (NOT / which conflicts with per-screen search bars)
     NOTE: Ctrl+K was DROPPED (conflicts with readline kill-to-end). Ctrl+P is the ONLY palette trigger.
   - Add . (period): Contextual action menu on selected item
   - Add Ctrl+T: Focus toast notification stack (NOT bare t which conflicts with per-screen keys)
   - Add Ctrl+S: Save filter preset (safe in raw-mode TUI; XOFF disabled)
   - Add Ctrl+L: Load filter preset
   - Add Space: Toggle item selection (batch mode)
   - Add Shift+A: Select all visible items
   - Add J (CAPITAL J): Toggle JSON tree view (NOT lowercase j which conflicts with help scroll + search nav)
   - Add Alt+Left: Back navigation (navigation stack)
   - Add Ctrl+N: Open compose panel
   - Add V (CAPITAL V): Events/Commits/Combined view toggle (Timeline screen only)
   - Add v: Enter/exit visual selection mode (batch screens)
   - Add g: Global/Local inbox toggle (Messages screen only)
   - Add n: Network graph toggle (Contacts screen only)

2. OPERATOR_RUNBOOK.md updates:
   - New section: Command Palette usage guide
   - New section: Toast notification configuration
   - New section: Filter presets
   - Updated: Screen descriptions with new widget capabilities

3. README.md TUI section:
   - Update keybinding table
   - Add command palette description
   - Add screenshot descriptions for new features

4. AGENTS.md TUI section:
   - Update screen count and descriptions
   - Add new env vars for toast configuration

## Acceptance Criteria
- All new keybindings documented with CORRECT keys (Ctrl+P not /, Ctrl+T not t, . not Enter, J not j, V not v for Timeline)
- Operator runbook covers all new features
- README and AGENTS.md updated","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T00:52:57.280011450Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T02:14:21.568931126Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.12","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T00:52:57.280011450Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.12","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T00:53:11.892739085Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-2bbt.13","title":"[track] Agent Communication Network Graph Visualization","description":"## Rationale (from webapp parity)

The webapp at GET /mail/archive/network features an interactive vis.js network graph showing:
- Nodes (agents) with scaling based on message volume
- Edges (message flows) with directional arrows
- Physics-based layout
- Hover tooltips with sent/received counts

The TUI has a Contacts screen (#11) but shows a flat table, not a graph.

## Widget Reference
- ftui_extras::canvas (Braille/HalfBlock/Block modes)
- NOTE: Canvas is FEATURE-GATED behind the "canvas" feature flag in ftui_extras.
  Must enable this feature in Cargo.toml: ftui_extras = { features = ["canvas"] }
- Painter API provides: draw_line, draw_circle, draw_rect, draw_text

## Implementation Approach

1. Add a Network sub-view to the Contacts screen (toggle with n key):
   - Use ftui_extras::canvas with Braille mode for highest resolution
   - Draw agent nodes as circles with size proportional to message count
   - Draw directed edges (arrows) between communicating agents
   - Edge thickness proportional to message volume between pair
   - Color coding: green=active contact, yellow=pending, red=blocked

2. Data source: query agent_links + message counts from DB
   - Cache graph layout (recompute only on data change)
   - Simple force-directed layout (or fixed grid for <20 agents)

3. Interaction:
   - Arrow keys to select/highlight a node
   - Enter on node to deep link to Agents screen (via br-2bbt.8)
   - Display tooltip with sent/received counts near selected node
   - Zoom in/out with +/- keys (changes canvas scale)

4. Fallback: At Tiny terminal size, show tabular view instead of graph

## Acceptance Criteria
- Network graph renders agent communication patterns visually
- Node sizing reflects message volume
- Edge directionality shows message flow
- Works at Compact and Large terminal sizes (Tiny falls back to table)
- Canvas feature flag enabled in Cargo.toml
- Unit tests for layout algorithm and data aggregation
- Snapshot tests for graph rendering with sample data","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:08:40.650225653Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T02:02:43.645953626Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["contacts","frankentui","tui","visualization"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.13","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:08:40.650225653Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"br-2bbt.14","title":"[track] Unified Cross-Project Inbox View","description":"## Rationale (from webapp parity)

The webapp at GET /mail provides a unified inbox across ALL projects with full-text search,
filter tabs, sorting, importance badges, thread grouping, and project context per message.

The TUI Messages screen only shows messages for one project at a time. Operators managing
multiple projects must switch between them manually. This is the single biggest capability
gap between web and TUI.

## EXISTING INFRASTRUCTURE NOTE

The Search screen already has a Scope facet: ScopeMode (Global/Project/Product). The Messages
screen does NOT have an equivalent — it always operates on one project. This track adds
a Global/Local toggle to the Messages screen specifically.

The product bus already has cross-project capabilities:
- search_messages_product, fetch_inbox_product, summarize_thread_product
These should be reused or extended rather than building from scratch.

## Implementation Approach

1. Add a new view mode to the Messages screen: Local (current) vs Global (cross-project)
   - Toggle with g key on Messages screen ONLY
   - In Global mode, add project column to message table
   - Global mode queries across all projects in the DB

2. Global search:
   - Search bar in Global mode searches all projects
   - Results show project context (slug) alongside message data
   - FTS query runs across all projects

3. Filters in Global mode:
   - All / Unread / Urgent tabs (same as webapp)
   - Filter by project
   - Sort by newest/oldest/sender/project

4. Performance: cursor-based pagination (50 per page), project metadata cached

## Acceptance Criteria
- g key toggles between Local and Global inbox views (Messages screen only)
- Global view shows messages from all projects with project column
- Search in Global mode returns results across all projects
- Pagination handles large result sets
- Unit tests for cross-project query construction
- E2E test: create 2 projects with messages, toggle Global, verify all visible","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:08:57.337667836Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T02:27:07.318807339Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cross-project","inbox","tui","webapp-parity"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.14","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:08:57.337667836Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"br-2bbt.14.1","title":"Add cross-project query methods to DB layer","description":"Add database query methods that operate across all projects:

1. fetch_inbox_global(limit, offset, filters) -> Vec<MessageRow> with project_slug joined
2. count_unread_global(agent_filter) -> per-project unread counts
3. search_messages_global(query, scope, sort) -> Vec<MessageRow> with project context

IMPORTANT: Reuse existing product bus infrastructure where possible.
The product bus already has cross-project capabilities:
- search_messages_product: searches across linked projects
- fetch_inbox_product: fetches inbox across product-linked projects
- summarize_thread_product: summarizes threads across products

The Global inbox queries may be able to extend these existing methods or share
their cross-project JOIN logic. The key difference is that product bus filters
by product linkage, while Global inbox shows ALL projects unconditionally.
Refactor the shared JOIN logic into reusable helpers if the code paths overlap.

These methods query across all projects in the DB. They join messages with projects
table to include project_slug in results. They respect the same filtering, sorting,
and pagination as per-project queries.

## Tests
- Unit: fetch_inbox_global returns messages from 3 test projects
- Unit: count_unread_global returns correct per-project counts
- Unit: search_messages_global respects scope filter across projects
- Unit: pagination (offset/limit) works correctly across project boundaries
- Unit: verify shared logic with product bus queries (no duplication)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:20:52.089383755Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:25:29.805436647Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T06:25:29.805416730Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cross-project","database","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.14.1","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt.14","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:20:52.089383755Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":375,"issue_id":"br-2bbt.14.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Completed: Cross-project query methods

### Functions implemented in queries.rs:

1. **fetch_inbox_global()** - Fetch inbox messages across ALL projects
   - Joins messages with projects table for project_slug
   - Supports urgent_only filter, since_ts filter, limit
   - Returns GlobalInboxRow with project context

2. **count_unread_global()** - Count unread messages per project
   - Groups by project_id and slug
   - Returns Vec<ProjectUnreadCount> ordered by unread_count DESC

3. **search_messages_global()** - FTS5 search across all projects
   - Uses FTS5 MATCH with bm25 ranking
   - Falls back to LIKE query on FTS failure
   - Returns GlobalSearchRow with project context

### Supporting structs:
- GlobalInboxRow: MessageRow + project_id + project_slug
- ProjectUnreadCount: project_id + project_slug + unread_count
- GlobalSearchRow: search result with project context

### Tests (4 passing):
- fetch_inbox_global_empty_database_returns_empty
- count_unread_global_empty_returns_empty
- search_messages_global_empty_corpus_returns_empty
- search_messages_global_empty_query_returns_empty

### Validation:
- cargo check -p mcp-agent-mail-db: PASS
- cargo fmt --check: PASS
- cargo test (4 tests): ALL PASS
","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:25:07Z"}]}
-{"id":"br-2bbt.14.2","title":"Add Global/Local mode toggle to Messages screen UI","description":"Modify the Messages screen to support Global and Local viewing modes:

1. Add mode state: enum InboxMode { Local(project_slug), Global }
2. g key toggles between Local and Global modes
3. Mode indicator in screen header: [Local: my-project] or [Global: all projects]
4. In Global mode:
   - Add project column (colored badge) to message table
   - Filter tabs (All/Unread/Urgent) filter across all projects
   - Sort dropdown adds By Project option
   - Search bar searches across all projects
5. In Local mode:
   - Behavior unchanged from current implementation
   - All existing per-project functionality preserved exactly

Transition: switching Global -> Local remembers last active project. Switching Local -> Global preserves current search/filter state.

## Tests
- Unit: mode toggle state machine (Local -> Global -> Local)
- Unit: Global mode adds project column to table schema
- Snapshot: Global mode header showing [Global: all projects]
- Snapshot: message table with project column badges in Global mode
- E2E: create 2 projects with messages, toggle g, verify cross-project results","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:20:52.284094811Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:26:11.345973537Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cross-project","tui","ui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.14.2","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt.14","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:20:52.284094811Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.14.2","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt.14.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:21:19.872406185Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"br-2bbt.14.2","title":"Add Global/Local mode toggle to Messages screen UI","description":"Modify the Messages screen to support Global and Local viewing modes:

1. Add mode state: enum InboxMode { Local(project_slug), Global }
2. g key toggles between Local and Global modes
3. Mode indicator in screen header: [Local: my-project] or [Global: all projects]
4. In Global mode:
   - Add project column (colored badge) to message table
   - Filter tabs (All/Unread/Urgent) filter across all projects
   - Sort dropdown adds By Project option
   - Search bar searches across all projects
5. In Local mode:
   - Behavior unchanged from current implementation
   - All existing per-project functionality preserved exactly

Transition: switching Global -> Local remembers last active project. Switching Local -> Global preserves current search/filter state.

## Tests
- Unit: mode toggle state machine (Local -> Global -> Local)
- Unit: Global mode adds project column to table schema
- Snapshot: Global mode header showing [Global: all projects]
- Snapshot: message table with project column badges in Global mode
- E2E: create 2 projects with messages, toggle g, verify cross-project results","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:20:52.284094811Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:34:42.350826493Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T06:34:42.350806005Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cross-project","tui","ui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.14.2","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt.14","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:20:52.284094811Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.14.2","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt.14.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:21:19.872406185Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":376,"issue_id":"br-2bbt.14.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Completed: Global/Local mode toggle for Messages screen

### Changes to messages.rs:

1. **InboxMode enum** - New enum with variants:
   - `Local(String)` - View messages from a single project
   - `Global` - View messages from ALL projects
   - Includes `label()` and `is_global()` helper methods

2. **MessageBrowserScreen new fields**:
   - `inbox_mode: InboxMode` - Current viewing mode
   - `last_local_project: Option<String>` - Remembers last project when toggling

3. **Key binding** - `g` key toggles between Local and Global modes:
   - Global → Local: Uses last known project or infers from focused message
   - Local → Global: Remembers current project for later restoration
   - Note: `Home` key now goes to start (previously shared with `g`)

4. **Mode indicator in search bar**:
   - Shows `[Local: project-slug]` or `[Global: all projects]` in header
   - Updated `render_search_bar()` to accept `mode_label` parameter

5. **Project column in Global mode**:
   - Added `show_project: bool` field to `MessageEntry`
   - When true, displays `[project-slug]` badge in results list
   - Project badge shows first 8 chars of project slug

6. **Query filtering**:
   - `fetch_recent_messages()` accepts optional project filter
   - `search_messages_fts()` accepts optional project filter
   - `count_messages()` counts by project when filtered
   - All queries respect inbox mode for scoping

### Tests added (7 new):
- `inbox_mode_default_is_global`
- `inbox_mode_label_global`
- `inbox_mode_label_local`
- `g_key_toggles_inbox_mode`
- `toggle_inbox_mode_remembers_last_project`
- `toggle_inbox_mode_infers_project_from_cursor`
- `keybindings_include_inbox_mode`

### Validation:
- cargo check -p mcp-agent-mail-server: PASS
- cargo fmt --check: PASS
- cargo test (62 tests): ALL PASS

### UI behavior:
- Mode indicator: `Search (N results) via FTS | [Global: all projects]`
- Project badge in results: `> !! #1234 12:34:56 [myproj  ] Subject line here`
- Toggle preserves search/filter state across mode switches

 {"id":"br-2bbt.15","title":"[track] Archive Browser with File Content Preview","description":"## Rationale (from webapp parity)

The webapp at GET /mail/archive/browser/{project_slug} provides a two-panel layout:
directory tree on left, file content on right, with auto-detection for markdown/JSON/text.

The TUI has no equivalent way to browse the Git archive. Operators must use the CLI or shell.

## Implementation Approach

1. Add a new screen (the TUI already has 14 screens in tui_screens/, adding one more):
   - tui_screens/archive_browser.rs (new file — genuinely new functionality)
   - Register in tui_screens/mod.rs as new MailScreenId::ArchiveBrowser variant
   - Left pane: directory tree of archive
   - Right pane: file content preview

2. Directory tree:
   - Use ftui_widgets::tree::Tree widget (verified to exist in frankentui)
   - Directories expandable/collapsible with Enter
   - Show file count per directory
   - Sort: directories first, then files alphabetically

3. Content preview pane:
   - .json files: syntax-highlighted JSON (use JSON tree widget from br-2bbt.7 if available)
   - .md files: rendered markdown (ftui_extras::markdown already used in TUI)
   - Other: plain text with line numbers
   - Show file metadata (size, last modified) in header

4. Navigation:
   - Arrow keys to navigate tree
   - Enter to expand directory or select file
   - Tab to switch focus between tree and preview
   - Esc to go up one directory
   - / to search file names within current directory

5. Data source: read from Git archive on disk (STORAGE_ROOT/{slug}/.archive/)
   - Cache directory listings (invalidate on archive write events)
   - Lazy-load file contents (only load when selected)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Two-panel archive browser renders correctly
- Directory tree shows archive structure using ftui_widgets::Tree
- File selection shows content preview with appropriate rendering
- Navigation works (expand/collapse, file select, pane switch)
- Works at Compact and Large terminal sizes
- Unit tests for directory listing, content type detection
- Snapshot tests for tree and preview rendering","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:09:15.411410587Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T02:28:29.346692073Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["archive","browser","tui","webapp-parity"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.15","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:09:15.411410587Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-2bbt.16","title":"[track] Activity Feed with Git Commit Timeline","description":"## Rationale (from webapp parity)

The webapp provides two complementary timeline views:

1. GET /mail/archive/activity - Activity Feed showing:
   - Recent commits across all projects
   - Stats bar (total commits, insertions, deletions, unique authors)
   - Commit timeline with visual connector lines
   - Commit hash, author/sender, recipients, date
   - Clickable links to commit details

2. GET /mail/archive/timeline - Communication Timeline showing:
   - Mermaid gitGraph visualization
   - Interactive commit list with sender->recipients flow

The TUI Timeline screen (#9) shows MailEvents but does NOT show Git commit history. Adding commit timeline data would give operators a complete picture of both messaging activity and archive writes.

## Implementation Approach

1. Extend the existing Timeline screen with a view toggle:
   - Events view (current): MailEvents from ring buffer
   - Commits view (new): Git commits from archive
   - Combined view: interleaved events + commits chronologically

2. View toggle key: V (CAPITAL V)
   KEYBINDING NOTE: lowercase v is reserved for visual selection mode (batch operations,
   br-2bbt.10) which also applies on the Timeline screen. Using CAPITAL V avoids this
   conflict. Capital V is mnemonic for "View mode" and follows the pattern of Capital J
   for JSON toggle and Shift+T for theme cycling.

3. Commit data source:
   - Query git log from archive repositories
   - Parse commit messages for sender, recipients, message IDs
   - Cache results (refresh on archive write events)
   - Cross-project: show commits from all project archives

4. Commit entry rendering:
   - Short SHA (colored), author, date, subject line
   - Sender -> Recipients flow indicator
   - File change stats (+insertions, -deletions)
   - Linkable: Enter on commit deep-links to Archive Browser (br-2bbt.15) at that path

5. Stats summary bar:
   - Total commits (today/week/all)
   - Total insertions / deletions
   - Unique authors count
   - Active projects count

6. Optional: Mermaid-style ASCII gitGraph using ftui_extras canvas
   - Simplified branch/merge visualization
   - Only if frankentui mermaid supports gitGraph format

## Acceptance Criteria
- Timeline screen has Events/Commits/Combined toggle (V key, CAPITAL V)
- Commits view shows git history with SHA, author, date, stats
- Combined view interleaves events and commits chronologically
- Stats summary bar shows aggregate commit metrics
- Enter on commit navigates to Archive Browser
- Cross-project commit aggregation works
- v (lowercase) still works for visual selection mode (no conflict)
- Unit tests for git log parsing, commit data aggregation
- Snapshot tests for commit entry rendering
- E2E test: create messages (which create archive commits), switch to Commits view, verify commits shown","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:09:32.930138037Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T02:14:41.672057742Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["archive","commits","timeline","tui","webapp-parity"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.16","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:09:32.930138037Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-2bbt.17","title":"[track] Human Overseer Message Compose Panel","description":"## Rationale (from webapp parity)

The webapp at GET /mail/{project_slug}/overseer/compose provides a message composition interface.
The TUI has no way for human operators to compose and send messages to agents.

## Implementation Approach

1. Add Compose overlay (Ctrl+N to open from any screen):
   - Floating panel (70% width, 80% height) with border
   - Z-order: above screens and toasts, same level as command palette

2. Fields using real frankentui widgets:
   - Project selector (if Global mode): TextInput with autocomplete
   - Recipients: multi-select list with checkboxes (compose from List + selection state)
     - Select All / Clear buttons
     - Filter agents by typing
   - To/Cc/Bcc toggle per recipient (default: To)
   - Subject: single-line TextInput (max 200 chars, with counter)
   - Body: ftui_widgets::textarea::TextArea (EXISTS in frankentui)
     - Multi-line editing with scroll
     - Tab to indent, Shift+Tab to outdent (within body field)
   - Importance: cycle through Urgent/High/Normal/Low with I key
   - Thread ID: optional TextInput (for replying to existing thread)

3. Actions:
   - Ctrl+Enter to send (with confirmation modal from br-2bbt.5)
   - Esc to close (with unsaved changes warning if body is non-empty)
   - Tab to cycle between fields

4. Send logic:
   - Call send_message tool internally (same code path as MCP tool)
   - Show success toast via existing NotificationQueue (br-2bbt.2)
   - Close compose panel on success

5. Sender identity: Use a special Overseer agent name
   - Auto-register Overseer agent on first compose
   - Clearly marked as human-originated in message metadata

## Widget References (CORRECT paths)
- TextArea: ftui_widgets::textarea::TextArea (for message body)
- TextInput: already used throughout TUI (for subject, thread ID)
- Toast: via existing NotificationQueue in MailAppModel

## File Guidance
Create tui_compose.rs (new file — compose panel is genuinely new functionality
with ~200+ lines that doesn't belong in any existing screen). Or add to tui_app.rs
if the overlay approach is simpler.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Ctrl+N opens compose panel overlay
- All fields work with correct frankentui widgets
- Multi-select recipient list with filtering
- Body uses real TextArea for multi-line editing
- Ctrl+Enter sends with confirmation modal
- Success toast shown after send
- Esc closes with unsaved changes warning
- Unit tests for compose state management, field validation
